critical_format_rules: NEVER use placeholder text like [Name], [Date], _____, XXXX, etc.
Use actual names, dates, amounts from the instructions provided
If exact date unknown, use 'on or about [month/year]' or describe contextually
If name unknown, use role descriptors ('the plaintiff', 'the defendant')
Document must be complete and ready to file - NO blanks
Output CLEAN MARKDOWN only - no HTML tags, no code fences
Use markdown pipe tables with |---| separator for ALL tabular data
Use **bold** for names and emphasis, ## headings for sections
MINIMIZE horizontal rules (---) - use ONLY between major sections, never between paragraphs
Output as SINGLE CONTINUOUS document without page breaks or section dividers

legal_notice.description: General Legal Notice (fallback for cease and desist, defamation, breach of contract on non-monetary obligations, Section 80 CPC notice, statutory show-cause, employment termination, IP infringement, trespass, nuisance). Demand / cheque bounce / eviction have their own dedicated agents.

structure.stationery:
Centered

stack: advocate name (BOLD), credentials, office address (2 lines), contact line (mob/email/enrl). All inline-styled HTML

with margin:0.25rem 0; line-height:1.375;.
structure.rpad_banner: BY REGISTERED POST A.D. / SPEED POST
structure.to_block:
Each line a standalone

with padding:0 3.5rem;. Recipient name in ; numbered 1, 2, 3 for multiple addressees.
structure.from_block: Sender (client) block with name. 'Through: [Advocate Name], Advocate' line at end.
structure.subject_line:
SUBJECT: ...

- precise, names statute and cause.
structure.opener:
Single

with the 'Under instructions from ...' opener naming the client (in ) and the advocate.
structure.body:
Numbered HTML

paragraphs (1, 2, 3, ...) - each with style="padding:0 3.5rem;". for emphasis. NO ## headings, NO --- rules, NO markdown numbered lists, NO em-dashes.
structure.legal_characterisation: Inline statement of statutory hooks under both old (IPC/CrPC) and new (BNS/BNSS/BSA) provisions where applicable.
structure.demand:
Inline TAKE NOTICE opener within a numbered

. Acts (a), (b), (c) listed inside the same

separated by semicolons. Time limit (typically 15 days; 2 months for Sec 80 CPC).
structure.consequences:
Inline TAKE FURTHER NOTICE opener within a numbered

. Specific civil + criminal proceedings my client will initiate.
structure.signature_block:
Stacked plain

: 'Issued under my hand and seal at [City] on this DD/MM/YYYY' (left-padded), 'Yours faithfully,', then RIGHT-aligned advocate name (BOLD), 'Advocate for [Client Name]', and 'Enrl. No.: ...'.
structure.copy_to:
Left-padded

Copy to:

followed by numbered list including 'My client - for information and record.'
structure.enclosures:
Left-padded

Enclosures:

followed by numbered supporting documents.
structure.mode_of_service: Mode of Service: By Registered Post Acknowledgement Due (R.P.A.D.) / Speed Post / Email at the above-mentioned address(es).

format_guidelines:
Body is HTML

blocks, NOT markdown numbered lists. Each carries style="padding:0 3.5rem;".

Use ... NOT **bold** inside

. Markdown emphasis is not parsed inside HTML.
ASCII hyphen-minus only. NEVER em-dash (-) or en-dash (-).
NO ## section headings inside the body. NO --- horizontal rules.
NO PRAYER, NO VERIFICATION - notices are not court filings.
All amounts in figures + words with Indian numbering: Rs. 4,25,000/- (Rupees Four Lakh Twenty-Five Thousand Only).
All dates in DD/MM/YYYY.
Statutory references in both old (IPC/CrPC) and new (BNS/BNSS/BSA) where applicable.
Time limit: 15 days standard; 2 months for Section 80 CPC notices to government.
Tone: formal, restrained, fact-led. No emotional adjectives or rhetorical exclamation.
legal_notice.example_snippet:
Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil

B.A., LL.B.

Office No. 14, 2nd Floor, Court Chambers,

Shivaji Nagar, Pune - 411005

Mob.: 9876543210 Email: skarale@advocate.in Enrl. No.: MAH/12345/2014

BY REGISTERED POST A.D. / SPEED POST

Dated: 12/05/2026

To,

1. M/s Solitaire Apparels Pvt. Ltd.

Through its Director Shri Anil Mehta

Plot 41, Industrial Estate, Hinjewadi Phase II,

Pune, Maharashtra - 411057

From,

Raymonds Limited (Denim Division)

Having its registered office at Pokhran Road No. 1, Thane West - 400606

Through: Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate

SUBJECT: Cease-and-desist Notice - Infringement of registered trademark 'RAYMONDS' (Reg. No. 1234567).

Dear Sir / Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Raymonds Limited, hereinafter referred to as "my client", I, Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate, do hereby serve upon you this Legal Notice and call upon you as follows:

1. My client, Raymonds Limited, is the registered proprietor of the trademark 'RAYMONDS' bearing Registration No. 1234567 dated 14/03/1992 in Class 25 of the Trade Marks Registry, Mumbai, in respect of textiles, garments, and apparel.

2. It has come to my client's knowledge that you have, on or about 02/04/2026, commenced manufacture, distribution, and sale of denim apparel bearing the deceptively similar mark 'RAYMOND'S DENIM CO.' through your retail and online channels, including via your website www.example-noticee.com, all without the authorisation of my client.

3. Your aforesaid acts constitute infringement of registered trademark under Section 29 of the Trade Marks Act, 1999, and amount to the tort of passing off; further, your conduct is also actionable as cheating under Section 318 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (corresponding to Section 420 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860).

4. TAKE NOTICE that you are hereby called upon: (a) to immediately and permanently cease and desist from all use of the impugned mark or any mark deceptively similar to my client's registered mark; (b) to deliver up for destruction all unsold goods, packaging, and promotional material bearing the impugned mark; (c) to render a written undertaking, on stamp paper of Rs. 500/-, that you shall not repeat the conduct aforesaid; and (d) to pay damages assessed at Rs. 25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Lakh Only) together with notice costs of Rs. 1,00,000/-; all within 15 (fifteen) days of the receipt of this notice.

5. TAKE FURTHER NOTICE that should you fail to comply, my client shall be constrained to institute (i) a Suit for permanent injunction, delivery up, rendition of accounts, and damages before the District Court / Commercial Court at Pune; and (ii) a Criminal Complaint under Sections 318 and 336 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, before the concerned Magistrate, all at your sole risk, cost, and consequence.

demand_notice.description: Demand Notice for recovery of money - unpaid invoices, undisputed dues, refund of advances, liquidated damages on breach of contract. NOT for cheque bounce (use ChequeBounceNoticeAgent) and NOT for eviction with rent arrears (use EvictionNoticeAgent).

structure.subject_line: Names the underlying instrument and the principal amount in figures + words.
structure.body_para_1: Client identity + commercial relationship + the SPECIFIC instrument that records the obligation (Purchase Order / Loan Agreement / MSA dated DD/MM/YYYY).
structure.body_para_2: The transaction in detail with dates and amounts in figures + words.
structure.body_para_3:
Particulars of dues - HTML borderless 2-column table (Particulars / Amount) with rows: Principal, Interest @ rate% from-to, Notice costs, Total. Below the table, a

aggregating the total in figures + words plus further interest until payment.
structure.body_para_4: Interest computation basis - contractual rate or Section 34 CPC.
structure.body_para_5: Default and prior demands - dates of reminders, addressee's silence.
structure.body_para_6: Legal characterisation - Section 73 Indian Contract Act + IPC/BNS where dishonest inducement disclosed.
structure.demand:
TAKE NOTICE opener. Acts (a), (b), (c) inside one

. Time limit: 15 days.
structure.consequences: TAKE FURTHER NOTICE opener. Order XXXVII summary suit (where applicable), arbitration (where contract has clause), Section 12A Commercial Courts Act mediation, criminal complaint where dishonest.
structure.copy_enclosures_mode: Same as legal_notice baseline. Enclosures: invoices / ledger / statement of accounts / bank statements.

format_guidelines:
Body is HTML

blocks with padding:0 3.5rem;. not **bold**. ASCII hyphens only.
Particulars table is HTML borderless (1px borders for cells, max-width 640px, centered).
Principal + interest computation must be reproducible from the table alone.
Time limit: 15 days from receipt (statutory default for non-Sec-138 demands).
Order XXXVII summary suit applies ONLY to written contracts / negotiable instruments / promissory notes for liquidated sums - omit if the underlying obligation does not qualify.
Indian numbering only: Rs. 4,25,000/- (Rupees Four Lakh Twenty-Five Thousand Only).
Aadhaar masked to last four (XXXX-XXXX-1234) if present.
NO PRAYER / VERIFICATION / ## headings / --- rules / em-dashes / markdown numbered lists.
demand_notice.example_snippet:
Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil

B.A., LL.B.

Office No. 14, 2nd Floor, Court Chambers,

Shivaji Nagar, Pune - 411005

Mob.: 9876543210 Email: skarale@advocate.in Enrl. No.: MAH/12345/2014

BY REGISTERED POST A.D. / SPEED POST

Dated: 12/05/2026

To,

1. FastShop Retail Pvt. Ltd.

Through its Director Mr. Anil Mehta

Plot 41, Industrial Estate, Hinjewadi Phase II,

Pune, Maharashtra - 411057

From,

Greentech Consulting Pvt. Ltd.

Having its registered office at 12, MG Road, Bangalore - 560001

Through: Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate

SUBJECT: Demand Notice - Recovery of Rs. 8,42,000/- (Rupees Eight Lakh Forty-Two Thousand Only) due under Master Services Agreement dated 04/01/2025.

Dear Sir / Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Greentech Consulting Pvt. Ltd., hereinafter referred to as "my client", I, Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate, do hereby serve upon you this Legal Notice and call upon you as follows:

1. My client, Greentech Consulting Pvt. Ltd., is engaged in the business of providing IT consulting services. You, FastShop Retail Pvt. Ltd., engaged my client to provide e-commerce platform development services pursuant to a written Master Services Agreement dated 04/01/2025.

2. Pursuant to the said Agreement, my client rendered services and raised invoices: Invoice No. GTC/2025/041 dated 31/03/2025 for Rs. 4,25,000/- (Rupees Four Lakh Twenty-Five Thousand Only) for Phase 1 (design); and Invoice No. GTC/2025/068 dated 30/04/2025 for Rs. 3,75,000/- (Rupees Three Lakh Seventy-Five Thousand Only) for Phase 2 (integration). Both invoices were duly delivered and acknowledged.

3. The particulars of the amount due and outstanding from you to my client are as follows:

Particulars Amount (Rs.)
Principal sum due under Invoices No. GTC/2025/041 and GTC/2025/0688,00,000/-
Interest @ 18% p.a. from 30/04/2025 to 12/05/2026 (377 days)37,000/-
Notice costs and incidental expenses5,000/-
Total amount due8,42,000/-

that is to say, a total sum of Rs. 8,42,000/- (Rupees Eight Lakh Forty-Two Thousand Only) as on 12/05/2026, together with further interest @ 18% per annum from 13/05/2026 until the date of actual payment.

4. Despite my client's reminders dated 15/05/2025, 12/06/2025, 20/07/2025, and the email dated 03/02/2026, you have failed to make payment and have remained in wilful default.

5. Your aforesaid non-payment constitutes a clear and continuing breach of contract under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, entitling my client to recover the outstanding sum together with interest, costs, and damages.

6. TAKE NOTICE that you are hereby called upon: (a) to pay to my client the total sum of Rs. 8,42,000/- together with further interest @ 18% per annum from 13/05/2026 until actual payment; (b) to pay notice costs of Rs. 5,000/-; and (c) to confirm such payment in writing, all within 15 (fifteen) days of the receipt of this notice.

7. TAKE FURTHER NOTICE that should you fail to comply, my client shall be constrained to institute a Summary Suit under Order XXXVII of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, before the Commercial Court at Bangalore, for recovery of the said sum together with interest, costs, and damages, all entirely at your risk and cost.

cheque_bounce_notice.description: Statutory Notice under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 - the mandatory pre-condition to a complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 NI Act before the competent Magistrate. Tight statutory regime: 30 days from receipt of Cheque Return Memo to despatch this notice; 15 days for the drawer to make payment from receipt.

structure.subject_line: Names Section 138 NI Act, the cheque amount in figures + words, the cheque number / date, and the drawee bank.
structure.body_para_1: Client identity + addressee + the legally enforceable debt for which the cheque was issued in discharge.
structure.body_para_2: Particulars of the cheque - HTML borderless 2-column table with rows: Cheque Number, Date of cheque, Amount, Drawer (you), Drawee bank and branch, Drawer's account number, Payee.
structure.body_para_3: Presentation date, presenting bank, Cheque Return Memo date and the EXACT bank-recorded reason for dishonour (in CAPS and <strong>).
structure.body_para_4: Recital of the legally enforceable debt - mandatory under Section 138 (the magic words: 'in discharge in whole or in part of a legally enforceable debt or other liability').
structure.body_para_5: Statutory characterisation - Section 138 + Section 139 presumption + Section 142(2) jurisdiction (post-2015 amendment).
structure.demand: TAKE NOTICE. Demand of EXACTLY the cheque amount (NOT principal + interest) within 15 days of receipt - statutory.
structure.consequences: TAKE FURTHER NOTICE. Criminal Complaint under Section 138 r/w Section 142 NI Act before the competent Magistrate at [City] within 30 days of expiry of the 15-day demand period.
structure.reservation_jurisdiction_signature: Per legal_notice baseline. Jurisdiction clause MUST reference Section 142(2) NI Act.
structure.enclosures: 1. Photocopy of dishonoured cheque. 2. Photocopy of Cheque Return Memo. 3. Optional: copy of underlying agreement / invoice / loan receipt.
structure.mode_of_service: Registered Post Acknowledgement Due (R.P.A.D.) MANDATORY - strongest proof of dispatch / receipt for Section 138 purposes.

format_guidelines: 30-day window to despatch from receipt of Cheque Return Memo - state both dates in body so compliance is evident.
Demand is the cheque amount EXACTLY - not principal + interest. Interest / damages claimed separately in civil proceedings.
15-day demand period is statutory - do not shorten.
Recite the legally enforceable debt with the magic words.
Identify the dishonour reason in the bank's exact words (CAPS, <strong>).
Jurisdictional clause MUST reference Section 142(2) NI Act (post-2015 amendment).
Account number masked to last four digits if input exceeds it.

Body is HTML

blocks with padding:0 3.5rem;. not **bold**. ASCII hyphens only.
NO PRAYER / VERIFICATION / ## headings / --- rules / em-dashes / markdown numbered lists.
Indian numbering only.
cheque_bounce_notice.example_snippet:
Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil

B.A., LL.B.

Office No. 14, 2nd Floor, Court Chambers,

Shivaji Nagar, Pune - 411005

Mob.: 9876543210 Email: skarale@advocate.in Enrl. No.: MAH/12345/2014

BY REGISTERED POST A.D. / SPEED POST

Dated: 12/05/2026

To,

1. Mr. Vikrant Anand Kulkarni

[Full Address - Bibwewadi, Pune]

From,

Mr. Sandeep Pradeep Joshi

Aged 42 years, occupation Business

Flat No. 304, Sai Krupa Apartments, Aundh, Pune - 411007

Through: Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate

SUBJECT: Statutory Notice u/s 138 NI Act, 1881 - Cheque No. 412567 dated 15/03/2026 for Rs. 5,00,000/-, dishonoured.

Dear Sir / Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Sandeep Pradeep Joshi, hereinafter referred to as "my client", I, Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate, do hereby serve upon you this Statutory Notice under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 and call upon you as follows:

1. My client advanced a friendly loan to you in the sum of Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakh Only) on 02/12/2025 in discharge of which you issued the cheque hereinafter described.

2. The particulars of the cheque issued by you to my client in discharge of the aforesaid liability are as follows:

Cheque Number412567
Date of cheque15/03/2026
AmountRs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakh Only)
Drawer (you)Mr. Vikrant Anand Kulkarni, individual
Drawee bank and branchHDFC Bank Ltd., Pune Camp Branch, MG Road, Pune - 411001
Drawer's account numberXXXXXX4287
PayeeMr. Sandeep Pradeep Joshi

3. The said cheque was deposited by my client for collection on 25/04/2026 at his banker, ICICI Bank Aundh Branch. The cheque was returned unpaid by HDFC Bank Pune Camp vide Cheque Return Memo dated 27/04/2026, with the endorsement 'FUNDS INSUFFICIENT'. My client received the said Memo on 29/04/2026.

4. The cheque aforesaid was issued by you in discharge in whole of a legally enforceable debt, namely, the friendly loan of Rs. 5,00,000/- advanced by my client to you on 02/12/2025, evidenced by the receipt dated 02/12/2025 signed by you and acknowledged via UPI transaction reference 524817642.

5. Your aforesaid act of issuing the dishonoured cheque constitutes an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881. The presumption under Section 139 is squarely available to my client; jurisdiction lies under Section 142(2) before the competent Magistrate at Pune.

6. TAKE NOTICE that you are hereby called upon to pay to my client the entire cheque amount of Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakh Only), by way of demand draft / RTGS / NEFT in favour of Mr. Sandeep Pradeep Joshi, within 15 (fifteen) days of the receipt of this notice, in terms of the proviso (c) to Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

7. TAKE FURTHER NOTICE that should you fail to make payment within 15 days of receipt, my client shall be constrained to file a Criminal Complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, before the competent Magistrate at Pune within 30 days of the expiry of the aforesaid 15-day period.

eviction_notice.description: Eviction Notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 (read with the applicable State Rent Control Act where relevant). Terminates the tenancy, calls upon the tenant to deliver vacant peaceful possession on a specific date, and demands arrears of rent + mesne profits.

structure.subject_line: Names Section 106 TP Act + State Rent Control Act (where applicable) and identifies the suit premises.
structure.body_para_1: Client identity + landlord status with documentary support (Sale Deed / Will / Gift Deed / Inheritance).
structure.body_para_2: Precise description of suit premises - flat/unit number, floor, carpet area, Survey No./CTS No., full address, boundaries.
structure.body_para_3: Tenancy particulars - mode of creation (registered Lease Deed / Leave & Licence / oral monthly), monthly rent in figures + words, security deposit, tenancy month definition.
structure.body_para_4: Default particulars - HTML borderless 2-column arrears table (Period / Rent), aggregating to total + further accruing rent until possession.
structure.body_para_5_onwards: Other grounds where applicable - unlawful sub-letting, change of user, structural alteration, nuisance, bona-fide requirement - each its own paragraph with State Rent Control Act citation.
structure.prior_demands: Dates of prior demands and addressee silence.
structure.statutory_characterisation: Termination under Section 106 TP Act read with State Rent Control Act. Continued occupation post-notice = trespass + mesne profits.
structure.demand: TAKE NOTICE. (a) deliver vacant possession on or before DD/MM/YYYY (last day of tenancy month falling 15 days after receipt for monthly tenancy / 6 months ending tenancy year for yearly); (b) pay arrears in figures + words; (c) pay mesne profits at market rate from notice expiry until possession; (d) notice costs; (e) settle municipal / electricity / water dues.
structure.consequences: TAKE FURTHER NOTICE. (i) Suit for Eviction before Court of Small Causes / Rent Controller / Civil Judge under Section 106 TP Act + State Rent Act; (ii) arrears + interest; (iii) mesne profits; (iv) costs and advocate fees. Reserve right to interim injunction.
structure.reservation_jurisdiction_signature: Per legal_notice baseline. Acceptance of any partial amount does NOT waive termination.
structure.enclosures: 1. Lease Deed / Leave & Licence Agreement. 2. Latest rent receipt or bank statement. 3. Document of title.

format_guidelines: Section 106 TP Act notice period: 15 days for monthly tenancy EXPIRING ON LAST DAY OF A TENANCY MONTH; 6 months for yearly tenancy expiring with end of tenancy year.
Compute the SPECIFIC calendar date for delivery of possession - do not write '15 days from receipt'.
Mesne profits claimed from EXPIRY of notice (not date of notice) until actual delivery of possession.
Cite both Section 106 TP Act + State Rent Control Act where the State is identifiable.
Property description sufficient for execution of a decree (Survey No./CTS No., boundaries).
No criminal threats - eviction notice is purely civil.

Body is HTML

blocks with padding:0 3.5rem;. not **bold**. ASCII hyphens only.
NO PRAYER / VERIFICATION / ## headings / --- rules / em-dashes / markdown numbered lists.
Indian numbering only.
eviction_notice.example_snippet:
Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil

B.A., LL.B.

Office No. 14, 2nd Floor, Court Chambers,

Shivaji Nagar, Pune - 411005

Mob.: 9876543210 Email: skarale@advocate.in Enrl. No.: MAH/12345/2014

BY REGISTERED POST A.D. / SPEED POST

Dated: 12/05/2026

To,

1. Mr. Omprakash Keshavrao Devgavdkar

Aged 50 years, occupation Business

Flat No. 426, Lotus Nandanvan Apartment, Moshi,

Pimpri-Chinchwad, Pune - 412105

From,

Mr. Jagdish Panditrao Choudhari

Aged 50 years, Manager Mechanical, Raymonds Ltd. (Denim Division)

41, Mahaveer Nagar Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal - 445001

Through: Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate

SUBJECT: Notice u/s 106 TP Act, 1882 - Termination of tenancy and demand for vacant possession of Flat 426, Lotus Nandanvan, Moshi.

Dear Sir / Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Jagdish Panditrao Choudhari, hereinafter referred to as "my client", I, Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil, Advocate, do hereby serve upon you this Legal Notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 read with the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, and call upon you as follows:

1. My client is the absolute owner of the suit premises by virtue of registered Sale Deed dated 18/06/2018 bearing Document No. 4521/2018 registered at the Office of the Sub-Registrar, Pimpri.

2. The premises in question (hereinafter the 'suit premises') are: Flat No. 426 admeasuring 717 sq. ft. carpet area, on the 4th floor of B Building No. 1, Lotus Nandanvan Apartment, Survey No. 274, Moshi, Pimpri-Chinchwad Municipal Corporation, District Pune - 412105.

3. By an oral monthly tenancy commencing 05/01/2022, my client let out the suit premises to you on the following terms: monthly rent of Rs. 8,500/- (Rupees Eight Thousand Five Hundred Only) payable on or before the 5th day of each English calendar month; refundable security deposit of Rs. 20,000/- received on 04/01/2022.

4. You have committed wilful and continuing default in payment of rent. The arrears as on 12/05/2026 are as follows:

Period Rent (Rs.)
05/03/2022 to 04/05/2026 (50 months)4,25,000/-
Interest @ 9% p.a. (computed)76,500/-
Total arrears5,01,500/-

that is to say, total arrears of Rs. 5,01,500/- (Rupees Five Lakh One Thousand Five Hundred Only) as on 12/05/2026, together with rent accruing from 13/05/2026 at the contractual rate until the date of delivery of vacant possession.

5. By reason of your aforesaid wilful default, the tenancy in respect of the suit premises stands forfeited. Without prejudice, my client hereby terminates the said tenancy under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 read with Section 16(1)(k) of the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999.

6. TAKE NOTICE that you are hereby called upon: (a) to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit premises to my client on or before 04/06/2026 (being the last day of the tenancy month falling next after fifteen days from receipt of this notice); (b) to pay arrears of rent in the sum of Rs. 5,01,500/-; (c) to pay mesne profits / damages for use and occupation from 05/06/2026 until actual delivery at the prevailing market rate of Rs. 18,000/- per month; and (d) to pay notice costs of Rs. 7,500/-.

7. TAKE FURTHER NOTICE that should you fail to comply, my client shall be constrained to institute a Suit for Eviction / Possession before the Court of Small Causes at Pune, together with claims for arrears of rent, mesne profits, and costs, all entirely at your risk and cost.

structure.parties:
**Plaintiff Name**
Age: XX yrs, Occ: [Occupation]
R/o: [Full Address]
Mob. no. [Number] ………Plaintiff

**Vs.**

**Defendant Name**
Age: XX yrs, Occ: [Occupation]

Mob. no. [Number] ……Defendant
structure.title: ## Affidavit For Plaint
structure.opening: I, [Full Name], Age [XX] years, residing at [Full Address], the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:
structure.body: Numbered paragraphs (1, 2, 3...) starting with 'I say that...' or 'That...' stating facts of the case
structure.jurisdiction: That the present court has jurisdiction to try this matter as [reason].
structure.closing: That I do hereby state on solemn affirmation that whatever is stated in the above paragraphs is true and correct to the best of my knowledge.
structure.signature_block:
Place: [City]
Date: ____/____/YYYY

Plaintiff      Advocate for the Plaintiff
         [Advocate Name]
structure.verification:
## VERIFICATION

I, [Full Name], Age: XX yrs, Occ: [Occupation], the Plaintiff in above matter, residing at [Address], do hereby state on solemn affirmation that what is stated in the above paragraphs no. 1 to [X] are true & correct to the best of my knowledge & information, which I believe to be true. Hence verified at [City] on this ___ day of ______YYYY.

Place: [City]
Date: ____/____/YYYY

Plaintiff

I know the Deponent.

Advocate for the Plaintiff

format_guidelines: Use 'I say that...' or 'That...' to begin each paragraph
State facts chronologically
Include specific dates, amounts, and addresses
Reference any prior legal notices or communications
State cause of action clearly
Include jurisdiction statement
End with verification on oath
Output clean MARKDOWN - no HTML tags

I, Shri Jagdish Panditrao Chaudhari, Age 50 years, residing at 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001, the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:

1. I say that I am the owner of the property situated at flat no. 426 measuring about 717 sq ft, in Lotus Nandanvan society, Moshi, Pimpri Chinchwad, Pune...

2. That the defendant was in need of a flat and therefore inquired with the security guard...

structure.opening: I, [Title] [Full Name], Age [XX] years, residing at [Full Address], the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:
structure.body: Numbered paragraphs (1, 2, 3...) with facts - each starting with 'I say that' or 'That'
structure.urgency: State urgency, bonafide requirement, and irreparable harm
structure.prayer_closing: **AND FOR WHICH ACT OF KINDNESS AND JUSTICE, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL AS IN DUTY BOUND EVER PRAY.**
structure.signature_block:
Place: [City]    Plaintiff    Advocate for the Plaintiff

structure.verification:
## VERIFICATION

Full verification paragraph
Place/Date + Plaintiff signature + 'I know the deponent.' + Advocate signature

format_guidelines: PARTY BLOCKS: Name in **bold**, details on separate lines, role marker (………Plaintiff) at end
ADDRESS FORMAT: Break into multiple lines - Street, Area, City, State - Pincode
NUMBERED PARAGRAPHS: Start with 'I say that' for first paragraph, 'That' for subsequent
AMOUNTS: Always write in figures AND words: Rs. 4,25,000/- (Rupees Four Lakh Twenty Five Thousand Only)
DATES: Use DD/MM/YYYY format for specific dates, 'on or about [month/year]' for approximate
Output clean MARKDOWN - no HTML tags
content_requirements: Para 1: State ownership/relationship to suit property
Para 2-N: Chronological facts of how dispute arose
State all communication attempts with defendant
Mention any legal notices sent/received with dates
State bonafide requirement with specific reasons (family need, medical, education)
State irreparable harm if relief not granted
State cause of action and when it arose
State jurisdiction
State amounts due with calculation (months x rent = total)
Final paragraph: Solemn affirmation of truth
interim_application.example_full_document:
# IN THE HON'BLE SMALL CAUSES COURT PUNE AT PUNE

**Civil Suit No.______ /2022**

**Jagdish Panditrao Choudhari**
Age: 50 yrs, Occ: Manager Mechanical
Raymonds Ltd. (Denim Division),
R/o: 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha
Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001
Mob. no. 9373188011        ………Plaintiff

**Vs.**

**Omprakash Keshavrao Devgavdkar**
Age: 50 yrs, Occ: Business,
R/o: Gate no. 274, Lotus Nandanvan Apartment,
Flat no. 426, B.no. 01, Moshi,
District Pune - 412105
Mob. no. 9769205996        …….Defendant

---

## Affidavit For Interim Application

I, Shri Jagdish Panditrao Chaudhari, Age 50 years, residing at 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001, the plaintiff, do hereby state on solemn affirmation as under:

1. I say that I am the owner of the property situated at flat no. 426 measuring about 717 sq ft, in Lotus Nandanvan society, Moshi, Pimpri Chinchwad, Pune and I am currently residing at 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal. I am filing the present affidavit for the purpose of stating the facts in the said application.

2. That the defendant was in need of a flat and therefore inquired with the security guard of the Lotus Nandanvan society, and was informed by the security guard of the Lotus Nandanvan society about my flat.

3. That the defendant approached me and requested me to provide the suit premises on leave and license basis till 04/01/2022, to which I agreed.

4. That the defendant inquired with me for the license fee for the suit property, to which I had informed about the deposit of Rs. 20,000/- (Rupees Twenty Thousand Only) and a monthly license fee of Rs. 8,500/- (Rupees Eight Thousand Five Hundred Only). That the defendant agreed to pay the deposit of Rs. 20,000/- to me by cash, and a monthly fee of Rs. 8,500/-.

22. That I do hereby state on solemn affirmation that whatever is stated in the above paragraphs is true and correct to the best of my knowledge.

**AND FOR WHICH ACT OF KINDNESS AND JUSTICE, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL AS IN DUTY BOUND EVER PRAY.**

---

Place: Pune      Plaintiff      Advocate for the Plaintiff
Date: ____/____/2022          Sattyajeet S. Karale Patil

---

## VERIFICATION

I, Mr. Jagdish Panditrao Choudhari, Age: 50 yrs, Occ: Manager Mechanical Raymonds Ltd. (Denim Division), R/o: 41, Mahaveer Nagar, Part 2, Darwha Road, Yavatmal, Maharashtra - 445001, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that what is stated in the above paragraphs no. 1 to 22 are true & correct to the best of my knowledge & information, which I believe to be true. Hence verified at Pune on this ___ day of ______2022.

Place: Pune
Date: ____ /___/2022        Plaintiff

I know the deponent.

Advocate for the Plaintiff

bail_application.description: Bail applications (regular, default) for criminal cases. Cause title is rendered separately by cause_title.py - this agent emits ONLY the body and signature blocks below.

structure.cause_title: Rendered deterministically by cause_title.py - court banner, MCRC No., applicant + State respondent blocks, document title. The agent does NOT emit any of these.
structure.custody_annotation:
Optional first

:

(Applicant in Jail)

- only when applicant is in custody.
structure.body_opener: The applicant most respectfully submits as under:
structure.body:
Flat numbered HTML

blocks, every one with style='padding:0 3.5rem;'. No ## or ### headings. No --- horizontal rules. No sub-numbering (1.1, 1.2). HTML for emphasis, NOT **markdown bold**.
structure.data_tables:
Bordered HTML for prior bail applications, case details, criminal antecedents, co-accused parity, legal authorities. Each preceded by an introductory numbered

. Omit table entirely when data is absent (state absence in prose).
structure.grounds: paragraphs continuing the body sequence. CATEGORY-OPENING paragraphs (those with the (A)/(B)/(C) Title Case label) do NOT carry a leading number - the (A)/(B)/(C) letter IS the marker. Continuation paragraphs within the same category keep the flat numbering, picking up from the last numbered paragraph. Result: 6, 7, (A) Title - substance, 8, 9, (B) Title - substance, 10, (C) Title - substance, 11, ...
structure.prayer:
Centered HTML heading:

PRAYER

. Reliefs as plain markdown (a), (b), (c).
structure.signature_block:
Stacked right-aligned

paragraphs with 3.5rem signature gaps. Place + Date left-aligned. Then 'Applicant' BOLD right-aligned (signature space above), applicant's typed name right-aligned plain immediately below. Then 'Advocate for the Applicant' BOLD right-aligned, advocate's typed name BOLD right-aligned. NO 3-column / NO table layout.
structure.verification:
Centered HTML heading:

VERIFICATION

with page-break-after:avoid. Body in

. Then Place + Date left, deponent role + name right-aligned (signature space), then 'I know the Deponent.' + 'Advocate for the [Role]' + advocate name LEFT-aligned with `padding:0 3.5rem;`.

format_guidelines: DO NOT emit the cause title (court banner, MCRC No., parties, document title) - the system prepends it.

Body paragraphs are HTML

blocks, numbered consecutively 1, 2, 3, ... - uniform 3.5rem padding on BOTH left and right.

Use ... inside

, NOT **markdown bold** - markdown emphasis is not parsed inside HTML blocks.

Grounds category labels are inline <strong> in Title Case - '(A) False Implication and Merits -' NOT '(A) FALSE IMPLICATION AND MERITS'. Category-opening paragraphs do NOT have a numeric prefix; only continuation paragraphs do. Numbering jumps over each (A)/(B)/(C) opener line.

Data tables are bordered HTML (1px solid #cbd5e1), each preceded by an introductory numbered

. Omit table entirely when data is absent.

PRAYER and VERIFICATION are centered + bold + underlined HTML

, NOT '## PRAYER' markdown headings.

Signature blocks are stacked right-aligned

with 3.5rem top-margin gaps between role label and the next signatory. Both the applicant's typed name and the advocate's typed name MUST appear below their respective role labels. NEVER use a 3-column layout or a .
Cite BOTH old law (CrPC/IPC) and new law (BNSS/BNS) for every section reference.
No '---' horizontal rules anywhere in the document.
content_requirements: FIR/Crime details with all section numbers (paragraph 2)
Date of arrest and period of custody
Prior bail history - table if any, prose 'no prior application' if none (paragraph 3)
Case details table (paragraph 4)
Criminal antecedents - table if any, prose 'no prior antecedents' if none (paragraph 5)
Co-accused parity table only if co-accused exist (paragraph 6, optional)
Brief prosecution case narrative (paragraph 7)
8-10 grounds with categorical Title Case labels (paragraphs 8 onwards)
Undertaking paragraph with conditions inline (a)-(e)
Legal authorities table if legal_case_search returned cases (optional, last body paragraph)
bail_application.example_snippet:
(Applicant in Jail)

The applicant most respectfully submits as under:

1. The applicant, Ramesh Kumar, S/O Shri Mohan Lal, aged about 32 years, Occupation: Farmer, R/o Village Barkheda, Tehsil Harda, District Harda, Madhya Pradesh - 461331, is a permanent resident of the said address and supports a family of five including his elderly parents, wife, and two minor children.

2. The applicant was arrested on 15/01/2025 in connection with Crime No. 142/2025 registered at Police Station Harda, District Harda, under Sections 303 and 34 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, corresponding to Sections 379 and 34 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860.

3. No prior bail application has been filed by the applicant before this Hon'ble Court, before any subordinate court, or before the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India in connection with the present Crime No.

4. The particulars of the case and the impugned remand order are as under:

Description of CrimeDetails of Impugned Order
Crime No. 142/2025, U/s 303, 34 BNS, PS Harda, Distt. Harda. Date of arrest: 15/01/2025. Chargesheet filed on 28/03/2025.B.A. No. 78/2025 - bail rejected by Sessions Judge, Harda vide order dated 20/01/2025.

5. The applicant has no prior criminal antecedents and has never been involved in any criminal case previously.

6. As per the prosecution case, the applicant is alleged to have committed the offence on the night of 14/01/2025 in Village Barkheda. The applicant categorically denies the said allegations and submits that he has been falsely implicated due to a long-standing land dispute with the complainant pending in Civil Suit No. 56/2023 before the Civil Judge, Harda.

(A) False Implication and Merits - The applicant is absolutely innocent. The prosecution case rests entirely on the testimony of the complainant and his close relatives, who have a direct enmity with the applicant arising from the pending land dispute. There is no independent witness to the alleged incident.

7. The contradictions between the FIR and the witness statements recorded under Section 161 CrPC go to the root of the prosecution case and render its narrative wholly unreliable.

(B) Investigation Complete - The investigation in the present case is complete and the chargesheet was filed on 28/03/2025. The continued custody of the applicant is no longer required for the purpose of investigation.

(C) Community Ties - No Flight Risk - The applicant is a permanent resident of Village Barkheda, where he owns 4 acres of ancestral agricultural land and supports a family of five. There is no likelihood whatsoever of the applicant absconding from justice.

(F) Constitutional Rights - Pre-trial incarceration in disregard of Article 21 of the Constitution of India is impermissible. Bail is the rule and jail is the exception, as held by the Hon'ble Supreme Court in Sanjay Chandra v. CBI - (2012) 1 SCC 40.

8. The applicant undertakes that if enlarged on bail, he shall (a) appear before the trial court on each and every date fixed without fail; (b) not leave the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court without prior permission; (c) not tamper with evidence or contact, intimidate, or influence any prosecution witness; (d) abide by all such conditions as this Hon'ble Court may impose.

PRAYER

It is, therefore, most humbly and respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may kindly be pleased to: (a) **Allow** this application and **enlarge the applicant on bail** in Crime No. 142/2025, Police Station Harda, District Harda, Madhya Pradesh, registered under Sections 303 and 34 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, pending trial; (b) Fix such conditions as this Hon'ble Court may deem appropriate; (c) Pass any other order as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the interest of justice.

Place: Harda

Date: 15/05/2025

Applicant

Ramesh Kumar

Advocate for the Applicant

Adv. Sunil Sharma

VERIFICATION

I, Ramesh Kumar, S/O Mohan Lal, aged 32 years, occupation Farmer, the Applicant in the above matter, residing at Village Barkheda, Tehsil Harda, District Harda, Madhya Pradesh - 461331, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that what is stated in the above paragraphs no. 1 to 8 is true and correct to the best of my knowledge and information, which I believe to be true. Hence verified at Harda on this 15 day of May, 2025.

Place: Harda

Date: 15/05/2025

Applicant

Ramesh Kumar

I know the Deponent.

Advocate for the Applicant

Adv. Sunil Sharma

hindi.example_snippet:
**म.अ.प्र. क्रमांक _______ / 2025**
**(धारा 439 दण्ड प्रक्रिया संहिता / धारा 483 भारतीय नागरिक सुरक्षा संहिता के अंतर्गत)**

**(आवेदक जेल में है)**

**रमेश कुमार** पुत्र श्री मोहन लाल
आयु: 32 वर्ष, निवासी ग्राम बरखेड़ा,
तहसील हरदा, जिला हरदा (म.प्र.)      ………आवेदक

**विरुद्ध**

मध्यप्रदेश शासन
थाना हरदा, जिला हरदा द्वारा      ………अनावेदक

---

| अपराध क्रमांक | थाना | धाराएं | प्राथमिकी दिनांक |
|------------|------|--------|----------------|
| 142/2025 | थाना हरदा | 303, 34 भा.न्या.सं. | 15/01/2025 |

---

**आधार:**

(I) यह कि आवेदक निर्दोष है तथा उसे प्रस्तुत प्रकरण में झूठा फंसाया गया है।

(II) यह कि विवेचना पूर्ण हो चुकी है एवं आरोप पत्र दाखिल किया जा चुका है। आवेदक की विवेचना हेतु आवश्यकता नहीं है।

(III) यह कि आवेदक की समाज में गहरी जड़ें हैं तथा वह फरार होने की स्थिति में नहीं है।

---

## प्रार्थना

अतः माननीय न्यायालय से सविनय प्रार्थना है कि आवेदक को अपराध क्रमांक 142/2025 थाना हरदा, जिला हरदा में नियमित जमानत प्रदान की जावे।

structure.brief_facts: Numbered paragraphs: FIR details, prosecution case, trial proceedings, evidence, defence, trial court findings
structure.grounds:
## GROUNDS OF APPEAL
Roman numerals (I), (II), (III)... challenging trial court findings

## PRAYER
Prayer to allow appeal, set aside conviction, acquit or reduce sentence

format_guidelines: Reference BOTH old (CrPC/IPC) and new (BNSS/BNS) statutory provisions
Use Roman numerals for grounds of appeal
Cite Supreme Court and High Court judgments on appellate review standards
Tables must use markdown pipe syntax with |---| separator rows
Distinguish between appeal against conviction vs. appeal against sentence
For SC/ST Act, NDPS, POCSO cases: cite specific provisions of those statutes
Output clean MARKDOWN - no HTML tags
content_requirements: Complete details of impugned judgment (court, case no., date, conviction, sentence)
FIR/Crime details with sections
Brief chronological facts of prosecution case
Summary of evidence led at trial
Defence version and trial court findings
At least 8-10 substantive grounds of appeal
Relevant case law citations
Clear prayer (acquittal or reduction of sentence)
criminal_appeal.example_snippet:
**CRIMINAL APPEAL No. _______ / 2025**
**(Under Section 374 CrPC / Section 415 BNSS)**

**Ramesh Kumar** S/o Shri Mohan Lal
Age: 45 years, R/o Village Barkheda,
District Harda (M.P.)      ………Appellant

**Vs**

State of Madhya Pradesh      ………Respondent

---

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Court | Sessions Court, Harda |
| Case No. | ST 50/2024 |
| Crime No. | 89/2023, PS Harda |
| Judgment Date | 10/12/2024 |
| Conviction | Sec. 302 BNS |
| Sentence | Life Imprisonment |

---

## GROUNDS OF APPEAL

(I) That the impugned judgment of conviction is against the weight of evidence on record.

(II) That the learned trial court failed to appreciate the material contradictions in the statements of prosecution witnesses.

(III) That the prosecution has failed to establish the chain of circumstances beyond reasonable doubt.

hindi.example_snippet:
**आपराधिक अपील क्रमांक _______ / 2025**
**(धारा 374 दण्ड प्रक्रिया संहिता / धारा 415 भारतीय नागरिक सुरक्षा संहिता के अंतर्गत)**

**रमेश कुमार** पुत्र श्री मोहन लाल
आयु: 45 वर्ष, निवासी ग्राम बरखेड़ा,
जिला हरदा (म.प्र.)      ………अपीलार्थी

**विरुद्ध**

मध्यप्रदेश शासन      ………प्रत्यर्थी

---

| न्यायालय | प्रकरण क्रमांक | अपराध क्रमांक | निर्णय दिनांक | दोषसिद्धि | दण्डादेश |
|---------|--------------|-------------|-------------|----------|--------|
| सत्र न्यायालय, हरदा | एस.टी. 50/2024 | 89/2023, थाना हरदा | 10/12/2024 | धारा 302 भा.न्या.सं. | आजीवन कारावास |

---

## अपील के आधार

(I) यह कि दोषसिद्धि का विवादित निर्णय अभिलेख पर उपलब्ध साक्ष्य के विरुद्ध है।

(II) यह कि विद्वान विचारण न्यायालय ने अभियोजन साक्षियों के कथनों में महत्वपूर्ण विरोधाभासों की सराहना करने में त्रुटि की है।

(III) यह कि अभियोजन परिस्थितियों की श्रृंखला को युक्तियुक्त संदेह से परे स्थापित करने में विफल रहा है।

---

## प्रार्थना

अतः माननीय न्यायालय से सविनय प्रार्थना है कि:
(अ) प्रस्तुत अपील स्वीकार की जावे;
(ब) सत्र न्यायालय, हरदा द्वारा एस.टी. 50/2024 में पारित दिनांक 10/12/2024 का दोषसिद्धि आदेश अपास्त किया जावे;
(स) अपीलार्थी को समस्त आरोपों से दोषमुक्त किया जावे।
